entries.value: Target MRR growth rate is >5% month-over-month. Below 3% signals stagnation. Above 10% indicates hypergrowth. Calculate as (MRR_current - MRR_previous) / MRR_previous * 100.

Target monthly churn rate is <5%. Best-in-class SaaS achieves <2% monthly churn. Calculate as churned_accounts / total_accounts_at_period_start * 100. Net revenue churn includes expansion revenue offsets.
Target NPS is >40. Scores 0-6 are detractors, 7-8 passive, 9-10 promoters. NPS = %promoters - %detractors. Above 50 is excellent. Below 0 requires urgent product/support intervention.
Target trial-to-paid conversion rate is >15%. Top SaaS products achieve 25-30%. Calculate as paid_conversions / trial_starts * 100 within the trial window. Track by cohort week for trend analysis.

Target feature adoption rate is >60% of accounts using core features within 30 days. Track per feature: activated_accounts / eligible_accounts * 100. Low adoption of a core feature may indicate UX issues or missing documentation.

Target time to value is <7 days from signup to first 'aha moment'. Measure as the median days between account creation and first meaningful action (defined per product). Longer TTV correlates strongly with higher churn.
Trigger an alert when any account shows 3+ churn risk signals: no login in 14 days, support ticket escalation, NPS detractor score, cancelled scheduled calls, reduced seat count.

Healthy SaaS businesses generate 20-30% of new ARR from expansion (upsells, cross-sells, seat additions). Track net revenue retention (NRR) — target >110%. NRR above 120% indicates strong product-market fit.
ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) = MRR * 12. Use MRR for monthly operational decisions and ARR for investor reporting and annual planning. Never annualize one-time revenue or usage-based overages.
Always analyze SaaS metrics by signup cohort (weekly or monthly). Blended averages hide trends. A rising blended churn rate might mask improving recent cohorts diluted by a bad legacy cohort.

Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) target is <1 hour for sev1/sev2 incidents. Measures elapsed time from incident detection to full resolution. Track by severity level — sev1 <30 min, sev2 <1 hour, sev3 <4 hours, sev4 <24 hours.

Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) target is <5 minutes. Measures elapsed time from failure occurrence to detection via monitoring or alerting. Low MTTD requires comprehensive observability — synthetic checks, anomaly detection, and real-user monitoring.

Target uptime is 99.95%, allowing a maximum of 21.9 minutes of unplanned downtime per month. Track as (total_minutes - downtime_minutes) / total_minutes * 100. Scheduled maintenance windows are excluded from uptime calculations.

Target is daily deployments to production. Higher deployment frequency correlates with smaller blast radius per deploy, faster feedback loops, and lower change failure rate. Track deploys per day per service.

Blog posts should target >3 minutes average read time and >3% click-through rate from search. Low engagement signals content-audience mismatch or weak titles. Track by content type — tutorials typically outperform announcements.

Target minimum of 2 quality content pieces per week. Consistency matters more than volume. Track pipeline health: ideas → drafts → published. A healthy pipeline has 3x more ideas than published pieces.

Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) payback period target is <12 months. Calculate as CAC / (MRR * gross_margin). Payback >18 months signals unsustainable growth. Segment by channel — product-led typically has the shortest payback.

LTV/CAC ratio target is >3x. Healthy range is 3-5x. Below 3x means acquisition is too expensive relative to customer value. Above 5x may indicate underinvestment in growth. LTV = ARPU * gross_margin / churn_rate.

Track average days per deal stage and total sales cycle length. Pipeline velocity = (number_of_deals * average_deal_value * win_rate) / sales_cycle_days. Increasing velocity without sacrificing win rate is the goal.
Sales win rate target is >25% of qualified opportunities. Track by deal source — inbound typically has higher win rates than outbound. Segment by deal size and plan tier for actionable insights.

Support first response time target is <2 hours during business hours. Critical priority tickets should receive a response within 30 minutes. Track by priority level and channel — chat and phone should be near-instant.

Support ticket resolution target is <24 hours for high priority and <4 hours for critical. Track median resolution time, not average, to avoid skew from outliers. Segment by category for process improvement.

Customer satisfaction score (CSAT) target is >4.2 out of 5. Measure after each resolved ticket. Scores below 3.5 should trigger a review of the support interaction. Track trends by agent, category, and priority.

Net Revenue Retention (NRR) target is >110%. NRR = (starting_MRR + expansion - contraction - churn) / starting_MRR * 100. NRR >100% means the business grows even with zero new customers. Best-in-class SaaS achieves >130%.

Gross Revenue Retention (GRR) target is >90%. GRR = (starting_MRR - contraction - churn) / starting_MRR * 100. GRR cannot exceed 100% (excludes expansion). Below 85% indicates a leaky bucket that growth cannot fill.

Expansion revenue target is >20% of total MRR growth. Track expansion sources: seat additions, plan upgrades, cross-sells, and usage overages. High expansion rates signal strong product-market fit and effective account management.
